mission.title: Install and Configure Chaos Mesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Chaos Mesh is an open source cloud-native Chaos Engineering platform that allows you to simulate various faults in Kubernetes environments. Installing Chaos Mesh enables you to orchestrate chaos experiments, helping to identify potential issues in your applications and improve their resilience.

steps.title: Add the Chaos Mesh Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Chaos Mesh Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add chaos-mesh https://charts.chaos-mesh.org
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Chaos Mesh using Helm
steps.description:
Install Chaos Mesh with the specified version and create a namespace for it:
```bash
helm install chaos-mesh chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh --version v2.8.1 --namespace chaos-mesh --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Chaos Mesh pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace chaos-mesh
```

steps.title: Configure RBAC for Chaos Mesh
steps.description:
Ensure that RBAC is enabled and configured by applying the following command:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh/master/deploy/rbac.yaml --namespace chaos-mesh
```

steps.title: Access the Chaos Dashboard
steps.description:
Port-forward the Chaos Dashboard service to access it locally:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/chaos-dashboard -n chaos-mesh 2333:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Chaos Mesh will have all pods running in the 'chaos-mesh' namespace, and you will be able to access the Chaos Dashboard at http://localhost:2333. You can now start creating and managing chaos experiments.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add chaos-mesh https://charts.chaos-mesh.org
helm repo update
helm install chaos-mesh chaos-mesh/chaos-mesh --version v2.8.1 --namespace chaos-mesh --create-namespace

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
